Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: codeaftermath <17331561+codeaftermath@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(monitor): use storageCapabilities config as authoritative signal for SPM availability (#3587)

## Problem
- Part of #3540
- Previous PR #3554 made the whole Monitor tab conditional on storage
support, but we want to keep the Empty state as advertisement of the
capabilities unless explicitly turned off in the config.

The Service Performance Monitoring (SPM) Monitor page had several
interconnected issues:

1. **Page crash on load** — `MonitorATMEmptyState` crashed with
`TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'mainTitle')`
because the embedded config provided a `monitor` key that completely
overwrote `defaultConfig.monitor` (including `emptyState`), due to a
shallow merge.

2. **Unnecessary API calls** — When metrics storage is not configured,
the Monitor page fired API requests on load and only showed the empty
state after receiving a `501 Not Implemented` response. The intent was
always to use `storageCapabilities.metricsStorage` from config as the
authoritative signal, not a runtime API probe.

3. **`storageCapabilities` in `jaeger-ui.config.json` was silently
ignored** — The Vite dev-server plugin injected the JSON config into
`getJaegerUiConfig()` but never into `getJaegerStorageCapabilities()`.
Since `index.html` always defines `getJaegerStorageCapabilities`
(hardcoded to return `metricsStorage: false`), the config file value was
always overwritten.

## Changes

### `default-config.ts` — add `monitor` to `mergeFields`

`mergeFields` controls which top-level config keys are shallow-merged
(rather than wholesale replaced) when the embedded config is combined
with defaults. `monitor` was missing, so a backend-provided `monitor: {
menuEnabled: true }` silently dropped `emptyState`, causing the crash.

### `vite.config.mts` — inject `storageCapabilities` into
`getJaegerStorageCapabilities()`

The Go query-service injects `storageCapabilities` into `index.html` via
a separate search-replace on `JAEGER_STORAGE_CAPABILITIES`. The Vite
plugin was only replicating the `JAEGER_CONFIG` injection. It now also
replaces `JAEGER_STORAGE_CAPABILITIES` when `storageCapabilities` is
present in `jaeger-ui.config.json`, making local dev fully equivalent to
production.

### `get-config.ts` — add comments clarifying the two-source design

`storageCapabilities` is intentionally loaded from a separate source
(`getJaegerStorageCapabilities`) and always overrides anything in the
main UI config, keeping the backend authoritative. This was not
documented; comments now explain the three-priority merge model.

### `Monitor/ServicesView/index.tsx` — gate on config, not runtime API
response

- Reads `storageCapabilities.metricsStorage` from config at render time.
- Returns `<MonitorATMEmptyState />` immediately when it is `false`,
with no API calls made.
- Removed the `isATMActivated !== false` guard from `fetchMetrics` (it
was checking a Redux flag that was only set after a 501 response).

### `reducers/metrics.ts` / `types/metrics.ts` — remove `isATMActivated`

`isATMActivated` was a runtime approximation of
`storageCapabilities.metricsStorage`: it started as `null`, then flipped
to `false` upon a `501` response. Now that the config is the
authoritative source (known at page load), this redundant state and the
501-detection logic have been removed entirely.

### Tests

- `get-config.test.js` — new `storageCapabilities` describe block
covering: backend injection, fallback to defaults, and precedence of
`getJaegerStorageCapabilities` over the UI config.
- `reducers/metrics.test.js` — removed `isATMActivated` assertions and
the dedicated "501 Not Implemented" test case.
- `Monitor/ServicesView/index.test.js` — updated `getConfigValue` mock
to return `true` for `storageCapabilities.metricsStorage`; empty-state
test now toggles the config mock instead of setting `isATMActivated:
false`.
- `Monitor/index.test.js` — added `getConfigValue` mock; updated
empty-state test accordingly.

## How the config loading works (for reference)

```
jaeger-ui.config.json
        │
        ▼
[dev] Vite plugin injects into index.html:
  • JAEGER_CONFIG            → consumed by getJaegerUiConfig()
  • JAEGER_STORAGE_CAPABILITIES → consumed by getJaegerStorageCapabilities()   ← was missing

[prod] Go query-service does the same search-replace

        │
        ▼
get-config.ts  assembles the final Config from three sources (lowest → highest priority):
  1. defaultConfig                 (compile-time defaults)
  2. getJaegerUiConfig()           (full UI config)
  3. getJaegerStorageCapabilities() (always wins for storageCapabilities)
```

## AI Usage in this PR (choose one)
See [AI Usage
Policy](https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING_GUIDELINES.md#ai-usage-policy).
- [ ] **None**: No AI tools were used in creating this PR
- [ ] **Light**: AI provided minor assistance (formatting, simple
suggestions)
- [ ] **Moderate**: AI helped with code generation or debugging specific
parts
- [x] **Heavy**: AI generated most or all of the code changes

---------

  windowsdesktop runtime tests

fix: recording VecDeque + server tab_locks leak + periodic cleanup

- recording.rs: Vec→VecDeque for O(1) frame eviction (was O(n) remove(0))
- state.rs: unregister_tab() now removes tab_locks entry (was leaked)
- state.rs: add purge_expired_locks() method
- serve.rs: spawn 60s periodic cleanup task for expired tab locks

Co-authored-by: strawgate <6384545+strawgate@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace schemas with anonymized repro that reliably triggers deadlock

The previous minimal schemas (4 subgraphs with circular @override) did not
produce enough error text to trigger the cli-table3 wordWrap deadlock.

This version uses ~290 entity types with @key(fields: "id") defined in both
subgraph-monolith and subgraph-nodes without @shareable, producing ~290+
composition errors. The combined error text volume triggers the expensive
string-width → emoji-regex code path that blocks the Node.js main thread.

Verified: `timeout 30 npx wgc@0.108.0 router compose -i compose.yaml`
exits with code 124 (killed by timeout = deadlock confirmed).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: cosmo-compose-deadlock-repro
repository.description: Minimal reproduction for wundergraph/cosmo#2619 — wgc router compose deadlock with 4+ subgraphs

Cycle 22 Phase 5: Vendor Account Settings (#328)

* Initial plan

* Implement Cycle 22 Phase 5 - VendorAccount, Saved Views, Notification Preferences

Phase 5 deliverables:
- VendorAccount Marten document initialized by VendorTenantCreated event
- SaveDashboardView / DeleteDashboardView commands + handlers
- UpdateNotificationPreferences with opt-out model (all enabled by default)
- HTTP endpoints: GET/POST/DELETE dashboard-views, GET/PUT preferences
- Settings page in Blazor WASM (notification toggles, saved views table)
- Settings link in MainLayout navigation + Dashboard quick actions
- 18 integration tests covering handlers, endpoints, multi-tenant isolation
- RabbitMQ queue subscription for vendor-portal-tenant-created

All 77 tests pass (59 existing + 18 new).

Co-authored-by: erikshafer <12145838+erikshafer@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address UX Engineer blocking findings B1, B2, B3

B1: Add helper text under each notification preference toggle
B2: Add confirmation dialog before deleting saved views
B3: Add semantic form grouping (role=group, aria-labelledby) for accessibility
Also: aria-label on saved views table (NB3)

Co-authored-by: erikshafer <12145838+erikshafer@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address QA Engineer findings - duplicate name guard, expanded test coverage

QA Required:
- #1: Duplicate view name constraint with 409 Conflict (matches feature file spec)
- #2: Write endpoints 401 coverage (POST/DELETE/PUT without JWT)
- #3: Cross-tenant DELETE isolation test

QA Recommended:
- #4: POST→GET round-trip verification
- #5: DELETE→GET round-trip verification
- #6: Account-exists-but-no-views path test
- #7: Full DashboardFilterCriteria 5-field serialization round-trip
- #8: UpdatedAt timestamp assertions

All 86 tests pass (59 existing + 27 new).

Co-authored-by: erikshafer <12145838+erikshafer@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PO feedback - Update CONTEXTS.md endpoints, feature file, retrospective, CURRENT-CYCLE

PO Required:
- #1: Update CONTEXTS.md endpoint URLs to match implementation (5 URLs)
- #2: Update feature file to reflect document storage (not domain events)

Also updated:
- CONTEXTS.md Phase 5 status and roadmap
- CURRENT-CYCLE.md to reflect Cycle 22 completion
- vendor-portal-event-modeling.md Phase 5 checklist
- cycle-22-retrospective.md with Phase 5 details, lessons, metrics, sign-offs

Co-authored-by: erikshafer <12145838+erikshafer@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix code review findings: XSS in MarkupString, trim-before-validation

- Use Message instead of MarkupString in delete confirmation dialog (prevents XSS)
- Move ViewName.Trim() before IsNullOrWhiteSpace validation check (logic bug)

Co-authored-by: erikshafer <12145838+erikshafer@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix MudBlazor 9.1.0 API compatibility - ValidateAsync, ShowAsync

commit.message:
fix: add schedule to improve.yml to pick up approved-for-work issues

GitHub Actions does not trigger workflows from label events caused by
other Actions (GITHUB_TOKEN restriction). Added daily 10am UTC schedule
(1hr after review at 9am) to poll for approved-for-work issues.

Also supports workflow_dispatch for manual triggering.
Retains label event trigger for cases where a human manually labels.
Idempotent: skips issues already marked in-progress.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Pre-release fixes: CI benchmark path, template version, CHANGELOG, README

- ci.yml: fix benchmark artifact path — was 'SageTUI.Benchmarks-report-full-compressed.json'
  (assembly name), now 'SageTUI.Benchmarks.BufferDiffBenchmarks-report-full-compressed.json'
  (correct BenchmarkDotNet class-based naming). Benchmark CI has never worked before this fix.
- Benchmarks/Program.fs: replace four BenchmarkRunner.Run<T>() calls with
  BenchmarkSwitcher.FromAssembly(...).Run(argv) — correct architecture for CLI invocation
- templates/sagetui-app/SageTuiApp.fsproj: Version 0.1.0 -> 0.9.0 (0.1.0 never existed
  on NuGet.org — scaffolded apps would fail to restore on day one)
- SageTUI.Tests/TemplateSmokeTests.fs: update version assertion to match 0.9.0
- CHANGELOG.md: add '## [0.9.0] - 2025-01-01' header; fix orphaned sentence fragment
  on line 103 (benchmark-regression job note was detached from its parent list item)
- README.md: add ## Testing section (TestHarness + TuiExpect API examples);
  fix 'Package version: 0.1.0' -> '0.9.0'
- SageTUI.Library.fsproj: update PackageReleaseNotes — 2,544 -> 2,551 tests,
  add drain overflow protection and TestHarness click simulation to notes
- gh-pages branch created (separate commit 4c5fe2e) to break the benchmark
  bootstrap deadlock (benchmark-regression job could never succeed without a baseline)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: CORS blocked in production due to missing FRONTEND_URL (#4)

* fix: add FRONTEND_URL to EnvConfig and fix CORS in production

CORS was silently blocking all requests from Vercel because FRONTEND_URL
was read directly from process.env (bypassing EnvConfig), and defaulted
to false when the variable was unset.

- Add frontendUrl to EnvConfig interface and loadEnv()
- Use env.frontendUrl in CORS origin config in app.ts
- Document FRONTEND_URL in .env.example
- Add frontendUrl to all test testEnv fixtures

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update .env.example

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update packages/backend/src/app.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
